danger_signals.visual_form: Stationary structures scattered across levels that must be destroyed
danger_signals.meaning: Primary threat targets; player must eliminate all bases to progress and spawn boss

danger_signals.visual_form: Unique large enemy entity that appears after all bases destroyed
danger_signals.meaning: Stage completion gate; represents escalated danger requiring defeat to advance

danger_signals.visual_form: Incoming fire from bases and enemies during stage navigation
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat to player ship; avoidance required to maintain lives

danger_signals.visual_form: Death of Opa-Opa ship results in loss of all equipped upgrades
danger_signals.meaning: Permanent consequence indicating danger state; forces gear reset on respawn

safety_signals.visual_form: Currency items dropped by defeated enemies, bases, and bosses
safety_signals.meaning: Resource accumulation signal; indicates opportunity for progression investment

safety_signals.visual_form: Marked balloons that trigger shop access when touched
safety_signals.meaning: Safe upgrade opportunity; designated safe zones for equipment exchange

safety_signals.visual_form: Purchasable equipment modifications in shop interface
safety_signals.meaning: Power progression indicators; visible safety buffer through gear enhancement

safety_signals.visual_form: Permanent flying speed enhancements purchased from shop
safety_signals.meaning: Accumulated safety; faster movement reduces vulnerability window

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Player-controlled entity distinct from all enemy bases and bosses

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Bases and boss entities across Fantasy Zone planets
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple boss designs per stage; replaced bosses in Master System version indicate faction variation
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Bases populate each stage level; visual stage identity determined by boss and base placement patterns

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Bases visible across level; scrolling camera movable left/right; stage loops
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player can navigate freely; no boss present; enemies spawn from bases
Boss must be defeated to advance; stage progression gate activated

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Boss entity removed; money drops; stage transition occurs
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player advances to next stage; new level layout appears

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Fixed camera; sequential rematch against all previous bosses before final boss
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: No base destruction phase; immediate boss succession; escalating challenge state

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Shop interface overlays gameplay; upgrade menu displayed with pricing
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Time-limited shopping; prices escalate with each purchase; single equipment type at a time
All equipped upgrades permanently lost; player returns to base ship configuration

environmental_storytelling.example: Eight distinct stages represent invasion across Fantasy Zone planet group; each stage has unique base and boss configuration
Each stage boss has unique visual design indicating different enemy faction units; boss rush finale shows progression from early to late-game threats
Player must spend earned money to enhance weapons and speed; mechanical progression mirrors survival adaptation to escalating enemy threat
Permanent upgrade loss on death creates visual stakes; empty shop screen on respawn indicates setback state
Non-final stages loop horizontally, indicating containment zones within invaded planets; player navigation freedom within constrained space

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Can move left/right freely; fires standard weapon; deploys bombs; navigates stage
Sprouts feet and walks; can re-engage flight; indicates movement mode variation
Fire projectiles; indicate stage incompleteness; spawn enemies
Cease firing; drop money; trigger boss appearance when all eliminated
Appears only after base destruction; must be defeated to progress; represents stage gatekeeper
Releases money; stage advances; disappears from level
Indicates base locations; provides strategic information; reduces uncertainty state

notes: Fantasy Zone is a 'cute 'em up' horizontally scrolling shooter with atypical shmup design. Key world-state communication occurs through base presence/absence as stage completion indicator, boss appearance as threat escalation, and equipment loss on death as consequence visualization. The shop system creates safe zones where upgrade state is visually managed. Stage looping in non-final levels suggests contained invasion scenarios. Boss rush finale (stage 8) communicates culmination through sequential rematch mechanic. Limited source documentation on specific visual details; analysis based on gameplay mechanics and structural design patterns described in Wikipedia article.
